title: FSH LH Menopause Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: FSH & LH Levels: Menopause vs. Perimenopause
description: Check your FSH and LH hormone levels during menopause and perimenopause. Free calculator with reference ranges and interpretation.
intro: Reference values in health come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO). They provide a framework for interpreting your results, but your personal health context comes first. FSH and LH levels differ significantly between menopause and perimenopause. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you updated.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **hormone reference table**. Direct application with your FSH and LH values. Identifies menopause vs. perimenopause based on hormone levels.
useCases: Technical calculations for FSH and LH hormone assessment
Validate test results before clinical discussion
Learning and teaching about menopausal hormone changes
Quick reference for menopause stage identification
explanation:
**Reference values in health** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). These vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They provide guidance; your personal medical history always takes priority.

## How it calculates

FSH and LH levels are compared against reference ranges for menopause and perimenopause stages.

This calculator applies the formula with your values, checking against typical ranges and alerting if values fall outside expected ranges.

## Normal reference ranges

| Parameter | Adult Normal Range |
|---|---|
| Blood Pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting Heart Rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| SpO2 | 95-100% |
| Fasting Glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total Cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical decisions (medical, financial, legal), consult with a qualified specialist. Data is current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: FSH above 25-30 IU/L sustained indicates menopause.

faq.q: What does LH measure during menopause?
faq.a: LH rises alongside FSH during menopause, though typically less dramatically.

faq.q: What's the difference between perimenopause and menopause?
faq.a: Perimenopause is the transition phase when FSH fluctuates, lasting several months to years before menopause.

faq.q: What are early signs of menopause?
faq.a: Hot flashes, night sweats, irregular periods, vaginal dryness, and mood changes are common symptoms.

faq.q: Should I use hormone replacement therapy?
faq.a: HRT benefits and risks are individual. Discuss options with your gynecologist based on your symptoms and health history.
12 consecutive months without a menstrual period indicates menopause has occurred.

faq.q: What's the average age for menopause?
faq.a: Most women experience menopause between ages 45-55. Before age 40 is considered premature menopause.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about my FSH/LH levels?
faq.a: Schedule a visit if your levels are outside normal ranges, you're experiencing symptoms, or have a family history of cardiovascular or metabolic disease.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
